intro: Leptoglottis hamata, commonly known as the "Talismán" or "Madre de Cacao", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Leptoglottis hamata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leptoglottis hamata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of teas, syrups, and topical creams.
